title: osteomeles cuneata
intro: Osteomeles cuneata, also known as the copperwood or island copperwood, is a small tree or shrub native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Osteomeles cuneata include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused oil, with some herbal preparations also using the leaves and bark to create topical creams and salves.
